```rust
        // torrc location
        config_dir.join("tor"),
        // Tor data
        data_dir.join("tor"),
        // Hidden service keys
        data_dir.join("tor").join("site"),
    ];

    for dir in &dirs {
        // Create directory
        tokio::fs::create_dir_all(dir).await
            .map_err(|e| anyhow!("create tor dir {:?}: {}", dir, e))?;
        // Enforce permissions even if dir already existed
        #[cfg(unix)]
        {
            use std::os::unix::fs::PermissionsExt;
            tokio::fs::set_permissions(dir, std::fs::Permissions::from_mode(0o700)).await
                .map_err(|e| anyhow!("chmod tor dir {:?}: {}", dir, e))?;
        }
    }

    Ok(())
}

// ensure_torrc creates torrc only if it doesn't exist (persistent)
// Returns true if file was created, false if it already existed
pub async fn ensure_torrc(path: &std::path::Path, content: &[u8]) -> anyhow::Result<bool> {
    // Ensure parent dir exists
    if let Some(parent) = path.parent() {
        tokio::fs::create_dir_all(parent).await
            .map_err(|e| anyhow!("create parent dir: {}", e))?;
    }

    // Check if torrc already exists - DON'T overwrite
    if path.exists() {
        // File exists - preserve it, just fix permissions
        #[cfg(unix)]
        {
            use std::os::unix::fs::PermissionsExt;
            tokio::fs::set_permissions(path, std::fs::Permissions::from_mode(0o600)).await
                .map_err(|e| anyhow!("chmod file: {}", e))?;
        }
        // Not created, already existed
        return Ok(false);
    }

    // File doesn't exist - create it
    tokio::fs::write(path, content).await
        .map_err(|e| anyhow!("write file: {}", e))?;

    // Enforce permissions
    #[cfg(unix)]
    {
        use std::os::unix::fs::PermissionsExt;
        tokio::fs::set_permissions(path, std::fs::Permissions::from_mode(0o600)).await
            .map_err(|e| anyhow!("chmod file: {}", e))?;
    }

    // Created new file
    Ok(true)
}

// update_torrc overwrites torrc with new content (for config changes)
// Only called when the operator explicitly updates Tor config in server.yml
pub async fn update_torrc(path: &std::path::Path, content: &[u8]) -> anyhow::Result<()> {
    // Ensure parent dir exists
    if let Some(parent) = path.parent() {
        tokio::fs::create_dir_all(parent).await
            .map_err(|e| anyhow!("create parent dir: {}", e))?;
    }

    // Overwrite file with new content
    tokio::fs::write(path, content).await
        .map_err(|e| anyhow!("write file: {}", e))?;

    // Enforce permissions
    #[cfg(unix)]
    {
        use std::os::unix::fs::PermissionsExt;
        tokio::fs::set_permissions(path, std::fs::Permissions::from_mode(0o600)).await
            .map_err(|e| anyhow!("chmod file: {}", e))?;
    }

    Ok(())
}

// ensure_tor_file creates/updates a generic Tor file with correct permissions
// Used for files other than torrc (keys, etc.)
pub async fn ensure_tor_file(path: &std::path::Path, content: &[u8]) -> anyhow::Result<()> {
    // Ensure parent dir exists
    if let Some(parent) = path.parent() {
        tokio::fs::create_dir_all(parent).await
            .map_err(|e| anyhow!("create parent dir: {}", e))?;
    }

    // Write file with restricted permissions
    tokio::fs::write(path, content).await
        .map_err(|e| anyhow!("write file: {}", e))?;

    // Enforce permissions (in case file existed with wrong perms)
    #[cfg(unix)]
    {
        use std::os::unix::fs::PermissionsExt;
        tokio::fs::set_permissions(path, std::fs::Permissions::from_mode(0o600)).await
            .map_err(|e| anyhow!("chmod file: {}", e))?;
    }

    Ok(())
}
```

| Rule | Description |
|------|-------------|
| **Create before write** | Dirs must exist before creating torrc or keys |
| **Permissions: 0700** | Tor directories are private (Unix: `rwx------`) |
| **Permissions: 0600** | Tor files are private (Unix: `rw-------`) |
| **Ownership** | Current user/group (NOT tor user, app owns everything) |
| **Enforce on exist** | Always chmod/chown even if dir/file already exists (Unix only) |
| **Idempotent** | Safe to call multiple times |
| **Windows** | chmod/chown skipped; Windows uses inherited ACLs from user profile |

**Tor File Permissions (Unix - Windows uses inherited ACLs):**

| File/Dir | Path | Permissions | Owner | Notes |
|----------|------|-------------|-------|-------|
| Config dir | `{config_dir}/tor/` | `0700` | app user | Server creates/enforces |
| torrc | `{config_dir}/tor/torrc` | `0600` | app user | Server generates |
| Data dir | `{data_dir}/tor/` | `0700` | app user | Server creates/enforces |
| Control socket | `{data_dir}/tor/control.sock` | `0600` | app user | Unix only |
| Site dir | `{data_dir}/tor/site/` | `0700` | app user | Server creates/enforces |
| Private key | `{data_dir}/tor/site/hs_ed25519_secret_key` | `0600` | app user | Tor creates |
| Public key | `{data_dir}/tor/site/hs_ed25519_public_key` | `0600` | app user | Tor creates |
| Hostname | `{data_dir}/tor/site/hostname` | `0600` | app user | Tor creates |
| PID file | `{data_dir}/tor/tor.pid` | `0600` | app user | |
| Log file | `{log_dir}/tor.log` | `0600` | app user | |


**Tor is configured via `server.yml` and CLI only. No REST API for Tor configuration.**

| Operation | CLI Command |
|-----------|-------------|
| View status | `{project_name} tor status` |
| Validate config | `{project_name} tor validate` |
| Restart Tor | `{project_name} tor restart` |
| Regenerate .onion address | `{project_name} tor regenerate` |
| Start vanity address search | `{project_name} tor vanity start` |
| Apply vanity address | `{project_name} tor vanity apply` |
| Import existing keys | `{project_name} tor import-keys <path>` |

## Behavior

| Scenario | Behavior |
|----------|----------|
| First run (tor found) | Tor starts, generates .onion address, keys saved to `{data_dir}/tor/site/` |
| Subsequent runs | Tor starts, loads existing keys, same .onion address |
| Tor binary not found | Log INFO, continue without Tor (not an error) |
| Regenerate address | Old keys deleted from `{data_dir}/tor/site/`, new .onion generated |
| Import keys | Keys replaced in `{data_dir}/tor/site/`, Tor restarts with new address |
| Network issues | Tor retries connection automatically |

## CLI

The `--status` command includes Tor status:

```
$ myapp --status

Server Status: Running
  Port: 8080
  Mode: production
  Uptime: 2d 5h 30m

Tor Hidden Service: Connected
  Address: abcd1234...wxyz.onion
```

### Status Fields

| Field | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| Server Status | Running, stopped, or starting |
| Port | Bind port |
| Mode | production or development |
| Uptime | Human-readable uptime |
| Tor | Connected/disabled and onion address |

---


# PART 32: CLIENT

## Overview

**client is REQUIRED for all projects.**

Every server MUST have a companion client. The client provides terminal-based access to all server functionality and is essential for:
- Scripting and automation
- Headless/SSH environments
- CI/CD pipelines
- Power users who prefer terminal

When a project includes a client, it provides a terminal-based interface for interacting with the server. The client supports both standard command-line usage and an interactive TUI (Terminal User Interface) mode.

| Attribute | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Default binary name | `{project_name}-cli` |
| Versioning | Same as main application |
| Build | Part of same Makefile (`make build` produces both binaries) |
| Config location (Unix) | `~/.config/{project_org}/{internal_name}/cli.yml` |
| Config location (Windows) | `%APPDATA%\{project_org}\{internal_name}\cli.yml` |

## Binary Naming Rules

**Same rules apply to ALL binaries (server, client). See PART 8 for full details.**

| Rule | Display | Internal |
|------|---------|----------|
| Binary name | ACTUAL name (`std::env::args().next()`) | Hardcoded `{project_name}` |
| `--help` output | Shows actual binary name | - |
| `--version` output | Shows actual binary name | - |
| User-Agent | - | `{project_name}-cli/{version}` (hardcoded) |
| Config paths | - | User-scope CLI paths under the invoking user's home/profile (see CLI directory rules below) |

```rust
// Display: use actual binary name
let binary_name = std::env::args().next()
    .and_then(|p| std::path::Path::new(&p).file_name().map(|n| n.to_string_lossy().into_owned()))
    .unwrap_or_default();

// Internal: hardcoded project name (compiled via env! macro or build.rs)
const PROJECT_NAME: &str = "{project_name}";
let user_agent = format!("{}-cli/{}", PROJECT_NAME, version);
```

## CLI Open API Access

**The server is an open API — no authentication required. The CLI connects to the server URL and calls endpoints directly.**

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `--server URL` | Server base URL (required if not in cli.yml) |

**Server sources (priority order):**
1. `--server` flag (explicit)
2. Environment variable: `{PROJECT_NAME}_SERVER`
3. Config file: `cli.yml` → `server: https://...`
4. Compiled default (if IDEA.md specifies one)

**Usage:**
```bash
{project_name}-cli list
{project_name}-cli --server https://api.example.com list
```

## CLI Config File Permissions

**`cli.yml` contains server connection config. Create with standard permissions:**

| Path | Required perms |
|------|----------------|
| `~/.config/{project_org}/{internal_name}/cli.yml` (Unix) | `0644` |
| `%APPDATA%\{project_org}\{internal_name}\cli.yml` (Windows) | Default ACL |

## CLI Auto-Update (Same Pattern as Server Self-Update)

**The CLI follows the same flow as the server's self-update (PART 22): check version via autodiscover, download from the server, verify SHA-256, atomic replace, restart.**

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 1. Discover | CLI's `/api/autodiscover` response includes `cli_versions: { "linux-amd64": {"version": "1.2.3", "sha256": "..."}, ... }` and `cli_min_version`. CLI checks on every start (it's short-lived; no separate poll loop needed) and additionally on `{project_name}-cli --update check`. |
| 2. Decide | If `current_version < cli_versions[os-arch].version`: prompt the user (interactive) OR auto-update silently (when `update.auto: true` AND non-interactive AND `--update yes` was passed earlier). If `current_version < cli_min_version`: refuse to make further requests until updated; print "this CLI is too old; the server requires {min_version} — run '{project_name}-cli --update yes' to upgrade." |
| 3. Download | Fetch `{base}/cli/binaries/{project_name}-cli-{os}-{arch}` over HTTPS. Save to a tmp path (`/tmp/{project_org}/{project_name}-XXXXXX/cli.update.tmp` per the spec's tmp-dir rules). |
| 4. Verify SHA-256 | Same `verify_checksum()` from PART 22 — match against the `sha256` from autodiscover. Mismatch → delete temp, abort with stderr error. |
| 5. Atomic swap | Same platform-specific `replace_binary()` from PART 22. The CLI is user-installed (typically `/usr/local/bin/` or `~/bin/`) — if the user lacks write permission to the install path, CLI prints "you do not have permission to update {binary_path}; ask your admin or move the binary to a writable path" and exits cleanly. |
| 6. Re-exec | After successful replace, CLI re-execs the new binary with the original argv to continue the in-progress command. (Server restarts via service manager; CLI just re-execs since it's foreground.) |

**Configuration (`cli.yml`):**

```yaml
update:
  # default false for CLI (interactive prompt unless explicitly opted in). Server default true.
  auto: false
  # CLI is short-lived; checks once per command. No background poll.
  check_interval: per_invocation
  channel: stable
```

**Server-side endpoints:**

| Endpoint | Method | Auth | Purpose |
|----------|--------|------|---------|
| `/api/autodiscover` | GET | None or bearer | Returns `cli_versions` (each entry has `version` + `sha256`) and `cli_min_version` alongside server info |
| `{base}/cli/binaries/{project_name}-cli-{os}-{arch}` | GET | None (public — CLIs are user-distributed) OR bearer if `cli.binary_download.require_auth: true` is set | Streams the binary |

**Why CLI download default is unauthenticated:** the CLI is the entry point for new users. Forcing auth on the download means they need a token before they can install the tool that obtains the token. Operators who run a private deployment can flip `cli.binary_download.require_auth` to `true`.

**Audit events (added to PART 11 taxonomy):**

| Event | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `cli.update_started` | CLI began an auto-update download |
| `cli.update_completed` | CLI restarted on new version |
| `cli.update_checksum_invalid` | SHA-256 didn't match the published checksum |
| `cli.update_forced` | CLI below `cli_min_version`; refused to make further requests until updated |
| `cli.token_revoked_detected` | CLI received `401 TOKEN_REVOKED`, exited / cleared cached token |


### Flag-to-Config Save Rules

**Flags only update config if current value is empty or invalid:**

```rust
// save_if_empty_or_invalid updates config only when appropriate
fn save_if_empty_or_invalid(
    current: &str,
    flag_value: &str,
    validate: impl Fn(&str) -> bool,
) -> String {
    // Flag not provided - keep current
    if flag_value.is_empty() {
        return current.to_string();
    }

    // Validate flag value
    if !validate(flag_value) {
        // Invalid flag value - keep current, log warning
        tracing::warn!("invalid flag value, keeping current config");
        return current.to_string();
    }

    // Current is empty - save new value
    if current.is_empty() {
        // Save to config
        return flag_value.to_string();
    }

    // Current is invalid - replace with valid flag value
    if !validate(current) {
        // Save to config
        return flag_value.to_string();
    }

    // Current is valid - use flag for session only, don't save
    // Use but don't persist
    flag_value.to_string()
}
```

| Current Config | Flag Value | Result |
|----------------|------------|--------|
| Empty | Valid | **Save to config** |
| Empty | Invalid | Keep empty, warn |
| Valid | Valid | Use flag (don't save) |
| Valid | Invalid | Keep current, warn |
| Invalid | Valid | **Save to config** |
| Invalid | Invalid | Keep current, warn |

**Example:**
```bash
# First run: no server configured
{project_name}-cli --server https://api.example.com list
# → Saves to cli.yml (was empty)

# Second run: server already configured
{project_name}-cli --server https://staging.example.com list
# → Uses staging for THIS command, but cli.yml still has api.example.com

# To permanently change: edit cli.yml directly
```

## Modes

### Automatic Mode Detection

**Smart display mode detection. NEVER implement UI mode flags:**

| NEVER Implement | Reason |
|-----------------|--------|
| `--tui` | Auto-detected |
| `--cli` | Auto-detected |
| `--gui` | Auto-detected |
| `--mode tui/cli/gui` | `--mode` is ONLY for `production`/`development` (app mode), NOT UI |
| `tui` subcommand | Auto-detected |

**Display mode is auto-detected from environment. Override via config only (not flags).**

**Config Override (cli.yml):**

```yaml
# cli.yml
display:
  # auto (default) - detect from environment
  # gui - force native GUI (fails if no display)
  # tui - force terminal TUI (fails if no TTY)
  mode: auto
```

| Config Value | Behavior |
|--------------|----------|
| `auto` (default) | Detect: GUI if display, TUI if terminal, error if neither |
| `gui` | Force GUI mode (error if no display available) |
| `tui` | Force TUI mode (error if no terminal available) |

**Why config override exists:**
- User prefers TUI even when GUI available (power users)
- User prefers GUI even when running from terminal
- Accessibility requirements
- Testing specific modes

**Auto-detection remains default.** Config override is for users who explicitly want to force a mode.

| Condition | Result |
|-----------|--------|
| `-h`, `--help` | Print help, exit (CLI only, never TUI) |
| `-v`, `--version` | Print version, exit (CLI only, never TUI) |
| Interactive terminal + no command | TUI mode |
| Interactive terminal + config flags only | TUI mode |
| Interactive terminal + command/args | CLI mode |
| Piped/redirected output | Plain output (no TUI) |
| Non-interactive (cron, scripts) | Plain output |

**Exit-immediately flags (NEVER launch TUI):**
```bash
# Print help, exit
{project_name}-cli -h
# Print help, exit
{project_name}-cli --help
# Print version, exit
{project_name}-cli -v
# Print version, exit
{project_name}-cli --version
```

**Config flags (still launch TUI):**
```bash
# TUI mode
{project_name}-cli
# TUI mode (with dev.yml)
{project_name}-cli --config dev
# TUI mode (with server)
{project_name}-cli --server https://example.com
# TUI mode (with token)
{project_name}-cli --token abc123
```

**Command/args (CLI mode):**
```bash
# CLI mode
{project_name}-cli list
# CLI mode (search)
{project_name}-cli rust tutorials
# CLI mode (paste file)
{project_name}-cli notes.txt
```

```rust
fn detect_mode(args: &[String]) -> &'static str {
    // Exit-immediately flags - never TUI
    for arg in args.iter().skip(1) {
        match arg.as_str() {
            "-h" | "--help" | "-v" | "--version" => return "cli",
            _ => {}
        }
    }

    // Not a terminal = plain output
    if !atty::is(atty::Stream::Stdout) {
        return "plain";
    }

    // Config-only flags don't prevent TUI
    let config_flags: std::collections::HashSet<&str> = [
        "--config", "--server", "--token", "--debug",
    ].iter().cloned().collect();

    for arg in args.iter().skip(1) {
        if !arg.starts_with('-') {
            // Command/arg provided
            return "cli";
        }
        let flag = arg.split('=').next().unwrap_or(arg);
        if !config_flags.contains(flag) {
            // Action flag
            return "cli";
        }
    }

    // No args or config-only = TUI
    "tui"
}
```

## Display Environment Detection (CLI-Specific)

**Uses `display::detect_display_env()` from PART 7 (`src/common/display/detect.rs`).**

**CLI binary uses the auto-detected display mode:**

```rust
// CLI uses display detection from common crate
let env = display::detect_display_env();

// Check auto-detected mode with clear helper methods
if env.is_auto_detect_display_mode_gui() {
    // Launch native GUI
} else if env.is_auto_detect_display_mode_tui() {
    // Launch bubbletea TUI
} else if env.is_auto_detect_display_mode_cli() {
    // Text output with colors
} else {
    // DisplayModeHeadless - error for CLI (requires interaction)
}
```

### CLI Display Mode Hierarchy

| DisplayMode | Priority | When Auto-Detected | CLI Binary Behavior |
|-------------|----------|-------------------|---------------------|
| **DisplayModeGUI** | 1 | Native display, no remote | Launch native GUI (GTK/Cocoa/Win32) |
| **DisplayModeTUI** | 2 | Interactive terminal | Launch bubbletea TUI |
| **DisplayModeCLI** | 3 | Command provided or piped | Text output with colors |
| **DisplayModeHeadless** | 4 | No display, no TTY | Error (CLI requires interaction) |

### Mode Selection by Binary

| Binary | GUI | TUI | CLI | Headless |
|--------|-----|-----|-----|----------|
| **Server** | Status window | Status banner | Commands | Default (daemon) |
| **CLI** | ✅ Full app | ✅ Full app (default) | ✅ Commands | ❌ Error |

**Server just shows status banners. CLI is the only full-featured TUI/GUI app.**

### First-Run / Double-Click Behavior

**What happens when user downloads a binary and runs it with no arguments:**

| Binary | First Run | Configuration Method |
|--------|-----------|---------------------|
| **Server** | Generate default `server.yml` if absent, show status banner | Edit `server.yml` directly |
| **CLI** | Setup wizard (GUI/TUI) | Setup wizard (no WebUI for CLI) |

### Server: Status Banner Only

**Server detects environment and shows appropriate status banner:**

| Environment | Banner Type |
|-------------|-------------|
| **Terminal** | Console banner (ASCII art, URLs, status) |
| **DE (has display)** | GUI status window (system tray, clickable URLs) |
| **Remote (SSH/Mosh)** | Console banner |
| **Container** | Log output only |
| **Headless/daemon** | Log to file |

**No built-in TUI/GUI wizard for the Server binary.** It just runs:
- **Server**: Configured by editing `server.yml` directly; generates a default `server.yml` on first run


### CLI: Full TUI/GUI App with Setup Wizard

**CLI is the ONLY binary with a full TUI/GUI application and setup wizard.**

Why CLI needs a setup wizard:
- No WebUI available for CLI configuration
- Must connect to a server to be useful
- Needs to store server URL and credentials

**CLI must work 100% with entire server functionality.**

### Container Environment (Docker/Incus/LXC)

**Containers have no interactive display.** Configuration via environment variables or mounted config.

| Detection | Method |
|-----------|--------|
| Docker | `/.dockerenv` exists |
| Incus/LXD | `container=incus` or `container=lxc` in environment |
| Podman | `container=podman` in environment |

**Container behavior:**

| Binary | Container Behavior | Configuration Source |
|--------|-------------------|---------------------|
| **Server** | Start immediately, log output | Env vars, mounted config, or defaults |
| **CLI** | Requires `--server` flag | Flag, env var, or mounted config |

### GUI/TUI Requirements for CLI (NON-NEGOTIABLE)

**CLI's GUI and TUI must be professional, fully functional interfaces:**

| Requirement | GUI | TUI |
|-------------|-----|-----|
| **Professional UI/UX** | Native look (GTK/Cocoa/Win32) | Clean bubbletea interface |
| **Full server functionality** | 100% feature coverage | 100% feature coverage |
| **Keyboard navigation** | Tab, Enter, Escape | Full keyboard support |
| **Real-time validation** | Input validation | Input validation |
| **Error handling** | Clear error dialogs | Clear error messages |
| **Accessibility** | Platform a11y support | Screen reader compatible |
| **Theming** | Follow system theme | Follow terminal colors |

**GUI must NOT be:**
- Electron or web-based (use native toolkits only)
- Feature-incomplete compared to TUI
- A wrapper around TUI (fully native)

### Display Detection

| Environment | Server | CLI |
|-------------|--------|-----|
| **Local display** | GUI status window | Full GUI app |
| **Terminal** | Console banner | Full TUI app |
| **Remote (SSH/Mosh)** | Console banner | Full TUI app |
| **Container** | Log output | Requires flags |
| **Headless** | Daemon/service | Error |

### CLI First-Run Flow

```
User double-clicks {project_name}-cli:

1. SETUP WIZARD (GUI or TUI based on environment)
   ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   │  {PROJECT_NAME} CLI Setup                                [X] │
   ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
   │                                                             │
   │  Connect to a {project_name} server:                         │
   │                                                             │
   │  Server URL:                                                │
   │  [https://                                           ] [?]  │
   │                                                             │
   │  Authentication (optional):                                 │
   │  API Token:                                                 │
   │  [                                                   ] [?]  │
   │                                                             │
   │  Options:                                                   │
   │    ☑ Save to configuration                                  │
   │    ☐ Set as default server                                  │
   │                                                             │
   │                       [Test Connection]                     │
   │                                                             │
   │  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  │
   │                              [Cancel]  [Connect →]          │
   └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

2. CONNECTION TEST
   - Validates server URL format
   - Tests connection to server
   - Validates authentication (if provided)
   - Shows server info (name, version)

3. NORMAL OPERATION
   - Launches full TUI or GUI application
   - User can now manage server via CLI interface
```

**Detection priority for setup wizard:**

```rust
fn select_setup_mode() -> SetupMode {
    let env = display::detect_display_env();

    // PRIORITY 1: Remote sessions ALWAYS use TUI
    // Even if X11 forwarding is available (DISPLAY set), we use TUI because:
    // - X11 forwarding is slow and laggy over network
    // - Mosh doesn't support X11 at all
    // - Users SSHing in expect terminal interfaces
    // - TUI provides better UX over remote connections
    if env.is_ssh || env.is_mosh {
        return SetupMode::TUI;
    }

    // PRIORITY 2: Local display → GUI
    // Only reaches here if NOT a remote session
    if env.has_display {
        return SetupMode::GUI;
    }

    // PRIORITY 3: Terminal without display → TUI
    if env.is_terminal {
        return SetupMode::TUI;
    }

    // No display, no terminal → cannot do interactive setup
    SetupMode::Error
}
```

**Why remote sessions skip GUI (even with X11 forwarding):**

| Reason | Explanation |
|--------|-------------|
| **Performance** | X11 over network is slow, laggy, and frustrating |
| **Mosh** | Doesn't support X11 forwarding at all |
| **User expectation** | SSH users expect terminal-based tools |
| **Reliability** | TUI works on any terminal, X11 can fail |
| **Bandwidth** | TUI uses minimal bandwidth vs GUI rendering |

**GUI Setup Wizard (Native - GTK/Cocoa/Win32):**

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  {PROJECT_NAME} Setup                                    [X] │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                             │
│  Welcome! Let's configure your server.                      │
│                                                             │
│  Server URL:  [https://                              ] [?]  │
│                                                             │
│  ☑ Save configuration                                       │
│  ☐ Start automatically on login                             │
│                                                             │
│                    [Test Connection]                        │
│                                                             │
│  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  │
│                              [Cancel]  [Continue →]         │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**TUI Setup Wizard (Terminal - Bubbletea):**

```
╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║                      {PROJECT_NAME} CLI SETUP                          ║
╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
║                                                                      ║
║   No server configured. Let's set one up!                            ║
║                                                                      ║
║   Server URL: [https://                                    ]         ║
║                                                                      ║
║   [ ] Save to config (recommended)                                   ║
║                                                                      ║
║                        [Test Connection]                             ║
║                                                                      ║
╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
```

**CLI Setup Wizard Flow:**

```
User double-clicks {project_name}-cli:

1. Check for config file (cli.yml)
   ├─ Config exists with valid server URL?
   │   └─ YES → Proceed to TUI/GUI app
   └─ NO config or no server URL?
       └─ Launch setup wizard

2. Setup wizard:
   a. Prompt for server URL
   b. Test connection to server
      ├─ Success → Save to config, proceed
      └─ Fail → Show error, retry or exit
   c. Optional: authenticate (if server requires)
   d. Save configuration

3. Launch full TUI/GUI application
```


**Platform-Specific Double-Click Behavior:**

| Platform | Server | CLI |
|----------|--------|-----|
| **Windows** | Console opens, banner shown, runs in foreground | Console opens, setup wizard or TUI |
| **macOS (Terminal)** | Banner shown, runs in foreground | Setup wizard or TUI |
| **macOS (Finder)** | ⚠️ Opens Terminal briefly, closes | ⚠️ Opens Terminal briefly, closes |
| **Linux (Terminal)** | Banner shown, runs in foreground | Setup wizard or TUI |
| **Linux (File Manager)** | Depends on DE settings | Depends on DE settings |

**macOS Finder Note:** Raw binaries don't persist when double-clicked from Finder. Users should either:
1. Run from Terminal: `./projectname-cli`
2. Use the `.app` bundle (if GUI build is available)
3. Install via Homebrew and run from Terminal

**Setup Wizard Implementation:**

```rust
// src/client/setup/wizard.rs

use anyhow::Result;

// run_setup_wizard launches interactive setup if no server configured
pub async fn run_setup_wizard() -> Result<()> {
    let cfg = config::load_cli_config();
    if let Ok(cfg) = cfg {
        if !cfg.server.is_empty() {
            // Config exists with server - skip wizard
            return Ok(());
        }
    }

    // Launch TUI setup wizard
    let result = tui::run_setup_model().await?;

    if result.cancelled {
        return Err(anyhow::anyhow!("setup cancelled"));
    }

    // Save configuration
    config::save_cli_config(&config::CLIConfig {
        server: result.server_url,
        // ... other settings from wizard
    })
}

// Called at CLI startup only
pub async fn ensure_configured() -> Result<()> {
    let cfg = config::load_cli_config().ok();
    if cfg.as_ref().map_or(true, |c| c.server.is_empty()) {
        return run_setup_wizard().await;
    }
    Ok(())
}
```

### GUI Mode Requirements

**When GUI mode is available, use NATIVE platform toolkit. No Electron, no web views.**

| Platform | Toolkit | Notes |
|----------|---------|-------|
| **Linux** | GTK4 or Qt6 | GTK4 preferred for GNOME, Qt6 for KDE |
| **macOS** | Cocoa (AppKit) | Native macOS UI via objc crate |
| **Windows** | Win32/WinUI | Native Windows UI |
| **BSD** | GTK4 or Qt6 | Same as Linux |

```rust
// src/client/gui/mod.rs
// GUI is conditionally compiled
// Build with: cargo build --release --features gui

pub fn is_gui_available() -> bool {
    let env = display::detect_display_env();
    env.has_display && !env.is_ssh && !env.is_mosh
}

pub fn launch_gui(config: &Config) -> anyhow::Result<()> {
    match std::env::consts::OS {
        "linux" | "freebsd" | "openbsd" | "netbsd" => launch_gtk_gui(config),
        "macos" => launch_cocoa_gui(config),
        "windows" => launch_win32_gui(config),
        _ => Err(anyhow::anyhow!("GUI not supported on this platform")),
    }
}
```

### Platform-Specific GUI Launchers

```rust
// --- gui_linux.rs ---
// #[cfg(all(target_os = "linux", feature = "gui"))]

use gtk4::prelude::*;
use gtk4::{Application, ApplicationWindow};

pub fn launch_gtk_gui(config: &Config) -> anyhow::Result<()> {
    let app = Application::builder()
        .application_id("{plist_name}.cli")
        .build();

    app.connect_activate(|app| {
        let win = ApplicationWindow::builder()
            .application(app)
            .title("{PROJECT_NAME} CLI")
            .default_width(800)
            .default_height(600)
            .build();

        // Build UI from config
        build_main_window(&win, config);

        win.show();
    });

    app.run_with_args(&[] as &[&str]);
    Ok(())
}

fn build_main_window(win: &ApplicationWindow, config: &Config) {
    use gtk4::{Box as GtkBox, HeaderBar, Orientation};

    // Main container
    let box_ = GtkBox::new(Orientation::Vertical, 10);
    box_.set_margin_top(10);
    box_.set_margin_bottom(10);
    box_.set_margin_start(10);
    box_.set_margin_end(10);

    // Header
    let header = HeaderBar::new();
    header.set_show_title_buttons(true);
    win.set_titlebar(Some(&header));

    // Content area - implement based on CLI functionality
    // ...

    win.set_child(Some(&box_));
}
```

### SSH/Mosh Detection

**Remote sessions ALWAYS use TUI or CLI. Never attempt GUI over SSH/mosh.**

```rust
fn is_remote_session() -> bool {
    // SSH detection
    if std::env::var("SSH_CLIENT").is_ok() || std::env::var("SSH_TTY").is_ok() {
        return true;
    }

    // Mosh detection
    if std::env::var("MOSH").is_ok() {
        return true;
    }

    // Check for SSH connection string
    if std::env::var("SSH_CONNECTION").is_ok() {
        return true;
    }

    false
}
```

## CLI/TUI/GUI Theming

**See PART 16 "Themes (NON-NEGOTIABLE - PROJECT-WIDE)" for the unified color palette.**

**CLI/TUI/GUI use the same `theme::ThemePalette` from `src/common/theme/colors.rs`.**

### CLI Theme Configuration

```yaml
# cli.yml - theme section (same format as server.yml)
theme:
  # dark (default), light, auto
  mode: auto
```

### TUI Styles from Palette

```rust
// src/client/tui/styles.rs

use ratatui::style::{Color, Style, Modifier};
use crate::common::theme;

// TUIStyles holds ratatui styles derived from ThemePalette
pub struct TUIStyles {
    pub base: Style,
    pub title: Style,
    pub selected: Style,
    pub error: Style,
    pub success: Style,
    pub warning: Style,
    pub muted: Style,
    pub border: Style,
}

pub fn styles_from_theme_palette(p: &theme::ThemePalette) -> TUIStyles {
    TUIStyles {
        base: Style::default()
            .fg(Color::from_str(&p.foreground).unwrap_or(Color::White))
            .bg(Color::from_str(&p.background).unwrap_or(Color::Black)),
        title: Style::default()
            .fg(Color::from_str(&p.primary).unwrap_or(Color::Cyan))
            .add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),
        selected: Style::default()
            .fg(Color::from_str(&p.background).unwrap_or(Color::Black))
            .bg(Color::from_str(&p.primary).unwrap_or(Color::Cyan)),
        error: Style::default().fg(Color::from_str(&p.error).unwrap_or(Color::Red)),
        success: Style::default().fg(Color::from_str(&p.success).unwrap_or(Color::Green)),
        warning: Style::default().fg(Color::from_str(&p.warning).unwrap_or(Color::Yellow)),
        muted: Style::default().fg(Color::from_str(&p.muted).unwrap_or(Color::DarkGray)),
        border: Style::default().fg(Color::from_str(&p.border).unwrap_or(Color::Gray)),
    }
}
```

### CLI Colored Output

```rust
// src/client/cli/output.rs

use crate::common::theme;

// Apply palette colors to CLI text output
impl Output {
    pub fn print_success(&self, msg: &str) {
        if self.colors {
            let (r, g, b) = hex_to_rgb(&self.palette.success);
            println!("\x1b[38;2;{};{};{}m{}\x1b[0m", r, g, b, msg);
        } else {
            println!("{}", msg);
        }
    }

    pub fn print_error(&self, msg: &str) {
        if self.colors {
            let (r, g, b) = hex_to_rgb(&self.palette.error);
            println!("\x1b[38;2;{};{};{}m{}\x1b[0m", r, g, b, msg);
        } else {
            println!("{}", msg);
        }
    }
}
```

## Responsive Layout

**ALL interfaces MUST look professional from mobile (40x10) to 16K displays (400+ columns).**

### Screen Size Categories

| Category | Columns | Rows | Examples | Layout |
|----------|---------|------|----------|--------|
| **Micro** | <40 | <10 | Phone portrait SSH | Vertical stack, no chrome |
| **Minimal** | 40-59 | 10-15 | Phone landscape SSH | Single column, minimal borders |
| **Compact** | 60-79 | 16-23 | Tablet SSH, small terminal | Compact layout, abbreviated |
| **Standard** | 80-119 | 24-39 | Standard terminal | Normal layout |
| **Wide** | 120-199 | 40-59 | Wide terminal | Sidebars, multi-column |
| **Ultrawide** | 200-399 | 60+ | Ultrawide monitor | Three-column, dashboards |
| **Massive** | 400+ | 80+ | 4K/8K/16K displays | Multi-pane, tile layout |

### GUI Responsive Layout

```rust
// GUI window scaling for high-DPI and large displays
fn calculate_gui_layout(width: u32, height: u32, dpi: f64) -> Layout {
    // Scale based on DPI
    // 96 DPI is baseline
    let scale = dpi / 96.0;

    // Base sizes (at 96 DPI)
    let base_min_width: u32 = 800;
    let base_min_height: u32 = 600;

    // Calculate scaled sizes
    let _min_width = (base_min_width as f64 * scale) as u32;
    let _min_height = (base_min_height as f64 * scale) as u32;

    // Determine layout mode
    // 4K+
    if width >= 3840 {
        Layout {
            mode: "dashboard",
            columns: 4,
            sidebar: true,
            sidebar_w: (300.0 * scale) as u32,
            font_scale: scale,
        }
    // 1440p/QHD
    } else if width >= 2560 {
        Layout {
            mode: "wide",
            columns: 3,
            sidebar: true,
            sidebar_w: (280.0 * scale) as u32,
            font_scale: scale,
        }
    // 1080p
    } else if width >= 1920 {
        Layout {
            mode: "standard",
            columns: 2,
            sidebar: true,
            sidebar_w: (250.0 * scale) as u32,
            font_scale: scale,
        }
    // 720p
    } else if width >= 1280 {
        Layout {
            mode: "compact",
            columns: 1,
            sidebar: true,
            sidebar_w: (200.0 * scale) as u32,
            font_scale: scale,
        }
    } else {
        Layout {
            mode: "mobile",
            columns: 1,
            sidebar: false,
            sidebar_w: 0,
            font_scale: scale,
        }
    }
}
```

### TUI Responsive Layout

**TUI uses `terminal::SizeMode` from common/terminal (see PART 7) for size detection.**

```rust
// src/client/tui/layout.rs

use crate::common::terminal;

// LayoutConfig provides TUI-specific layout settings based on SizeMode
pub struct LayoutConfig {
    pub show_borders: bool,
    pub show_header: bool,
    pub show_footer: bool,
    pub show_sidebar: bool,
    pub sidebar_width: u16,
    pub max_columns: u8,
    pub truncate_at: u16,
    pub use_abbrev: bool,
    pub vertical_scroll: bool,
    pub multi_pane: bool,
    pub tile_layout: bool,
}

// get_layout_config returns layout config for a SizeMode
pub fn get_layout_config(mode: terminal::SizeMode) -> LayoutConfig {
    match mode {
        terminal::SizeMode::Micro => LayoutConfig {
            show_borders: false,
            show_header: false,
            show_footer: false,
            show_sidebar: false,
            max_columns: 2,
            truncate_at: 30,
            use_abbrev: true,
            vertical_scroll: true,
            ..Default::default()
        },
        terminal::SizeMode::Minimal => LayoutConfig {
            show_borders: false,
            show_header: true,
            show_footer: true,
            show_sidebar: false,
            max_columns: 3,
            truncate_at: 40,
            use_abbrev: true,
            vertical_scroll: true,
            ..Default::default()
        },
        terminal::SizeMode::Compact => LayoutConfig {
            show_borders: true,
            show_header: true,
            show_footer: true,
            show_sidebar: false,
            max_columns: 4,
            truncate_at: 60,
            use_abbrev: false,
            vertical_scroll: true,
            ..Default::default()
        },
        terminal::SizeMode::Standard => LayoutConfig {
            show_borders: true,
            show_header: true,
            show_footer: true,
            show_sidebar: false,
            max_columns: 6,
            truncate_at: 80,
            use_abbrev: false,
            vertical_scroll: true,
            ..Default::default()
        },
        terminal::SizeMode::Wide => LayoutConfig {
            show_borders: true,
            show_header: true,
            show_footer: true,
            show_sidebar: true,
            sidebar_width: 30,
            max_columns: 8,
            truncate_at: 120,
            use_abbrev: false,
            vertical_scroll: true,
            ..Default::default()
        },
        terminal::SizeMode::Ultrawide => LayoutConfig {
            show_borders: true,
            show_header: true,
            show_footer: true,
            show_sidebar: true,
            sidebar_width: 40,
            max_columns: 12,
            truncate_at: 200,
            use_abbrev: false,
            // Full content visible
            vertical_scroll: false,
            multi_pane: true,
            ..Default::default()
        },
        terminal::SizeMode::Massive => LayoutConfig {
            show_borders: true,
            show_header: true,
            show_footer: true,
            show_sidebar: true,
            sidebar_width: 50,
            max_columns: 20,
            // No truncation
            truncate_at: 0,
            use_abbrev: false,
            vertical_scroll: false,
            multi_pane: true,
            tile_layout: true,
        },
    }
}
```

### Window Resize Handling

**ALL modes MUST handle window resize smoothly.**

```rust
// TUI resize handling with ratatui/crossterm
impl Model {
    pub fn handle_event(&mut self, event: Event) -> anyhow::Result<Option<Action>> {
        match event {
            Event::Resize(width, height) => {
                self.width = width;
                self.height = height;

                // Use common/terminal for size mode detection
                let size = terminal::get_terminal_size();
                self.size_mode = size.mode;
                self.layout = get_layout_config(size.mode);

                // Recalculate all component sizes
                self.recalculate_viewport();
                self.recalculate_tables();
                self.recalculate_sidebar();

                // Log resize in debug mode
                if self.debug {
                    tracing::debug!("Window resize: {}x{}, mode: {:?}", width, height, self.size_mode);
                }
            }
            _ => {}
        }
        Ok(None)
    }
}

// Server: Handle window resize for console output
// Platform-specific implementations below

// --- watch_unix.rs ---
// #[cfg(not(target_os = "windows"))]

pub async fn watch_window_size(
    ctx: tokio::sync::CancellationToken,
    callback: impl Fn(u16, u16) + Send + 'static,
) {
    use tokio::signal::unix::{signal, SignalKind};
    let mut sig = signal(SignalKind::window_change()).expect("invariant: signal handler registration succeeds at startup");

    loop {
        tokio::select! {
            _ = ctx.cancelled() => return,
            _ = sig.recv() => {
                if let Ok((w, h)) = crossterm::terminal::size() {
                    callback(w, h);
                }
            }
        }
    }
}

// --- watch_windows.rs ---
// #[cfg(target_os = "windows")]

pub async fn watch_window_size(
    ctx: tokio::sync::CancellationToken,
    callback: impl Fn(u16, u16) + Send + 'static,
) {
    // Windows: Poll for size changes (no SIGWINCH)
    let mut interval = tokio::time::interval(std::time::Duration::from_millis(500));
    let (mut last_w, mut last_h) = (0u16, 0u16);

    loop {
        tokio::select! {
            _ = ctx.cancelled() => return,
            _ = interval.tick() => {
                if let Ok((w, h)) = crossterm::terminal::size() {
                    if w != last_w || h != last_h {
                        last_w = w;
                        last_h = h;
                        callback(w, h);
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    }
}
```

## Professional UI/UX Standards

**Every interface MUST look professional. No amateur hour.**

### Visual Hierarchy

| Element | Priority | Styling |
|---------|----------|---------|
| **Primary Actions** | High | Primary color, prominent placement |
| **Data/Content** | High | Clear contrast, readable fonts |
| **Navigation** | Medium | Consistent position, clear labels |
| **Secondary Actions** | Medium | Muted styling, discoverable |
| **Status/Meta** | Low | Muted color, smaller text |
| **Decorative** | Lowest | Subtle, never distracting |

### Spacing and Alignment

```rust
// Consistent spacing units (TUI)
// Micro spacing
pub const SPACE_XS: u16 = 1;
// Small spacing
pub const SPACE_S: u16 = 2;
// Medium spacing
pub const SPACE_M: u16 = 4;
// Large spacing
pub const SPACE_L: u16 = 6;
// Extra large spacing
pub const SPACE_XL: u16 = 8;

// Apply spacing based on terminal size mode
pub fn get_spacing_for_mode(m: terminal::SizeMode) -> u16 {
    match m {
        terminal::SizeMode::Micro | terminal::SizeMode::Minimal => SPACE_XS,
        terminal::SizeMode::Compact => SPACE_S,
        terminal::SizeMode::Standard => SPACE_M,
        terminal::SizeMode::Wide => SPACE_L,
        // Ultrawide, Massive
        _ => SPACE_XL,
    }
}
```

### Consistent Icons and Symbols

```rust
// TUI_SYMBOLS - Unicode symbols that work across terminals
pub struct TUISymbols {
    pub success: &'static str,
    pub error: &'static str,
    pub warning: &'static str,
    pub info: &'static str,
    pub arrow: &'static str,
    pub check: &'static str,
    pub cross: &'static str,
    pub bullet: &'static str,
    pub spinner: &'static [&'static str],
}

pub const TUI_SYMBOLS: TUISymbols = TUISymbols {
    success: "✓",
    error: "✗",
    warning: "⚠",
    info: "ℹ",
    arrow: "→",
    check: "☑",
    cross: "☒",
    bullet: "•",
    spinner: &["⠋", "⠙", "⠹", "⠸", "⠼", "⠴", "⠦", "⠧", "⠇", "⠏"],
};

// TUI_SYMBOLS_ASCII - Fallback for non-Unicode terminals
pub const TUI_SYMBOLS_ASCII: TUISymbols = TUISymbols {
    success: "[OK]",
    error: "[ERR]",
    warning: "[WARN]",
    info: "[INFO]",
    arrow: "->",
    check: "[x]",
    cross: "[ ]",
    bullet: "*",
    spinner: &["|", "/", "-", "\\"],
};

pub fn get_tui_symbols(env: &DisplayEnv) -> &'static TUISymbols {
    if env.supports_unicode() {
        &TUI_SYMBOLS
    } else {
        &TUI_SYMBOLS_ASCII
    }
}
```

### Standard CLI Mode

When command arguments are provided:

```bash
# Standard CLI output (your project)
{project_name}-cli create --file notes.txt --expire 24h
{project_name}-cli get abc123 --output json
{project_name}-cli list --limit 10
{project_name}-cli search --query "keyword"
```

### TUI Mode (Automatic)

When launched with no arguments in an interactive terminal:

```bash
# Launch TUI (no arguments needed)
# Opens TUI automatically
{project_name}-cli

# TUI provides:
# - Interactive menus
# - Search with live results
# - Keyboard shortcuts
# - Visual data display
# - Professional, polished interface
```

**TUI Library:** Use `ratatui` + `crossterm` for TUI implementation.

## Configuration

### Directory Structure

**client runtime state is always user-scope. The CLI binary may be installed system-wide, but config/data/cache/logs MUST stay in the invoking user's XDG/profile directories.**

The CLI binary:
- Runs in the invoking user's context without privilege escalation
- Uses user/home/profile directories exclusively, even if the invoking user happens to be root/Administrator
- May be installed in a shared system PATH location (for example `/usr/local/bin`) so one binary is available to all users
- Creates all directories and files itself (no external scripts/installers)
- Sets permissions and ownership before creating any files
- Works identically across all platforms (Linux, macOS, Windows)

The client uses the same user directory structure as the server in user mode. This allows the client to share configuration with a locally running server when appropriate.

#### Linux / macOS

| Directory | Path | Purpose |
|-----------|------|---------|
| Config | `~/.config/{project_org}/{internal_name}/` | Configuration files |
| Config File | `~/.config/{project_org}/{internal_name}/cli.yml` | CLI configuration |
| Data | `~/.local/share/{project_org}/{internal_name}/` | Persistent data |
| Cache | `~/.cache/{project_org}/{internal_name}/` | Temporary/cached data |
| Logs | `~/.local/log/{project_org}/{internal_name}/` | Log files |
| Log File | `~/.local/log/{project_org}/{internal_name}/cli.log` | CLI log output |

#### Windows

| Directory | Path | Purpose |
|-----------|------|---------|
| Config | `%APPDATA%\{project_org}\{internal_name}\` | Configuration files |
| Config File | `%APPDATA%\{project_org}\{internal_name}\cli.yml` | CLI configuration |
| Data | `%LOCALAPPDATA%\{project_org}\{internal_name}\data\` | Persistent data |
| Cache | `%LOCALAPPDATA%\{project_org}\{internal_name}\cache\` | Temporary/cached data |
| Logs | `%LOCALAPPDATA%\{project_org}\{internal_name}\log\` | Log files |
| Log File | `%LOCALAPPDATA%\{project_org}\{internal_name}\log\cli.log` | CLI log output |

#### Directory Usage

| Directory | Contents | Backup? |
|-----------|----------|---------|
| Config | `cli.yml`, connection profiles, preferences | Yes |
| Data | Downloaded data, local databases, saved items | Yes |
| Cache | API response cache, temp files, thumbnails | No (recreatable) |
| Logs | `cli.log`, debug logs | Optional |

**NEVER use OS system directories for CLI runtime state:**
- `/etc/{project_org}/{internal_name}/` (Linux system config)
- `/var/lib/{project_org}/{internal_name}/` (Linux system data)
- `/var/log/{project_org}/{internal_name}/` (Linux system logs)
- `C:\ProgramData\` (Windows system data)
- Any other system-owned directory for CLI config/data/cache/logs

#### CLI Startup Sequence

**The CLI binary handles ALL initialization. No external scripts, no installers, no manual setup.**

On every startup, the CLI MUST:

1. **Ensure directories exist** (create if missing):
   ```
   ├─ {config_dir}/           (~/.config/{project_org}/{internal_name}/)
   ├─ {data_dir}/             (~/.local/share/{project_org}/{internal_name}/)
   ├─ {cache_dir}/            (~/.cache/{project_org}/{internal_name}/)
   └─ {log_dir}/              (~/.local/log/{project_org}/{internal_name}/)
   ```

2. **Set correct permissions** (user-only access):
   - Directories: `0700` (rwx------)
   - Config files: `0600` (rw-------)
   - Log files: `0600` (rw-------)

3. **Verify ownership** (current user):
   - All directories and files owned by the invoking user account for that run
   - Never require system ownership or privileged/system directories just because the invoking user is root/admin

```rust
// src/client/init.rs

use std::fs;
use std::path::Path;
use anyhow::Result;

// ensure_dirs creates all CLI directories with correct permissions.
// Called on every startup before any file operations.
pub fn ensure_dirs() -> Result<()> {
    let dirs = [
        paths::config_dir(),
        paths::data_dir(),
        paths::cache_dir(),
        paths::log_dir(),
    ];

    for dir in &dirs {
        fs::create_dir_all(dir)
            .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("create dir {}: {}", dir.display(), e))?;
        // Set permissions (platform-specific)
        set_dir_permissions(dir)
            .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("set permissions {}: {}", dir.display(), e))?;
    }
    Ok(())
}

// --- permissions_unix.rs ---
// #[cfg(not(target_os = "windows"))]

pub fn set_dir_permissions(dir: &Path) -> Result<()> {
    use std::os::unix::fs::PermissionsExt;
    // Unix: ensure 0700 (user-only access)
    fs::set_permissions(dir, fs::Permissions::from_mode(0o700))?;
    Ok(())
}

pub fn set_file_permissions(path: &Path) -> Result<()> {
    use std::os::unix::fs::PermissionsExt;
    // Unix: ensure 0600 (user-only read/write)
    fs::set_permissions(path, fs::Permissions::from_mode(0o600))?;
    Ok(())
}

// --- permissions_windows.rs ---
// #[cfg(target_os = "windows")]

pub fn set_dir_permissions(_dir: &Path) -> Result<()> {
    // Windows: ACLs are inherited from parent by default in user directories
    // %APPDATA% and %LOCALAPPDATA% already have user-only access
    // No explicit ACL modification needed for user directories
    Ok(())
}

pub fn set_file_permissions(_path: &Path) -> Result<()> {
    // Windows: files inherit ACLs from directory
    // For sensitive files, we could set explicit ACLs, but for user
    // directories this is not needed as they inherit from %APPDATA%
    Ok(())
}

// ensure_file creates parent dirs and sets permissions before writing.
// MUST be called before any file creation.
pub fn ensure_file(path: &Path) -> Result<()> {
    if let Some(dir) = path.parent() {
        fs::create_dir_all(dir)
            .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("create parent dir: {}", e))?;
    }
    Ok(())
}
```

**CLI initialization order:**
1. Parse CLI flags
2. Call `ensure_dirs()` - create all directories
3. Load/create config file (`cli.yml`)
4. Initialize logging (`cli.log`)
5. Execute command

#### Path Resolution (Rust)

```rust
// src/client/paths/mod.rs

use std::path::PathBuf;

const PROJECT_ORG: &str = "{project_org}";
const PROJECT_NAME: &str = "{project_name}";

// config_dir returns the CLI config directory
pub fn config_dir() -> PathBuf {
    #[cfg(target_os = "windows")]
    {
        let appdata = std::env::var("APPDATA").unwrap_or_default();
        PathBuf::from(appdata).join(PROJECT_ORG).join(PROJECT_NAME)
    }
    #[cfg(not(target_os = "windows"))]
    {
        let home = dirs::home_dir().unwrap_or_default();
        home.join(".config").join(PROJECT_ORG).join(PROJECT_NAME)
    }
}

// data_dir returns the CLI data directory
pub fn data_dir() -> PathBuf {
    #[cfg(target_os = "windows")]
    {
        let localappdata = std::env::var("LOCALAPPDATA").unwrap_or_default();
        PathBuf::from(localappdata).join(PROJECT_ORG).join(PROJECT_NAME).join("data")
    }
    #[cfg(not(target_os = "windows"))]
    {
        let home = dirs::home_dir().unwrap_or_default();
        home.join(".local").join("share").join(PROJECT_ORG).join(PROJECT_NAME)
    }
}

// cache_dir returns the CLI cache directory
pub fn cache_dir() -> PathBuf {
    #[cfg(target_os = "windows")]
    {
        let localappdata = std::env::var("LOCALAPPDATA").unwrap_or_default();
        PathBuf::from(localappdata).join(PROJECT_ORG).join(PROJECT_NAME).join("cache")
    }
    #[cfg(not(target_os = "windows"))]
    {
        let home = dirs::home_dir().unwrap_or_default();
        home.join(".cache").join(PROJECT_ORG).join(PROJECT_NAME)
    }
}

// log_dir returns the CLI log directory
pub fn log_dir() -> PathBuf {
    #[cfg(target_os = "windows")]
    {
        let localappdata = std::env::var("LOCALAPPDATA").unwrap_or_default();
        PathBuf::from(localappdata).join(PROJECT_ORG).join(PROJECT_NAME).join("log")
    }
    #[cfg(not(target_os = "windows"))]
    {
        let home = dirs::home_dir().unwrap_or_default();
        home.join(".local").join("log").join(PROJECT_ORG).join(PROJECT_NAME)
    }
}

// config_file returns the CLI config file path
pub fn config_file() -> PathBuf {
    config_dir().join("cli.yml")
}

// log_file returns the CLI log file path
pub fn log_file() -> PathBuf {
    log_dir().join("cli.log")
}
```

### --config Flag (Config File Selection)

**The `--config` flag loads a specific config file instead of the default `cli.yml`.**

| Input | Resolution |
|-------|------------|
| `--config test` | `{config_dir}/test.yml` |
| `--config dev.yml` | `{config_dir}/dev.yml` |
| `--config test.yaml` | `{config_dir}/test.yaml` |
| `--config ~/testing/app.yml` | Absolute path: `~/testing/app.yml` |
| `--config /etc/myapp/prod.yml` | Absolute path: `/etc/myapp/prod.yml` |
| (not specified) | Default: `{config_dir}/cli.yml` |

**Resolution rules:**
1. If path is absolute (starts with `/` or `~`) → expand, then check extension
2. If path is relative → resolve relative to `{config_dir}`
3. If extension is `.yml` or `.yaml` → use as-is
4. If no extension → auto-detect: check `.yml` first, then `.yaml`, default to `.yml`
5. File must exist or error (except for new configs which default to `.yml`)

```rust
// resolve_config_path resolves --config flag to absolute path
fn resolve_config_path(config_flag: &str) -> anyhow::Result<PathBuf> {
    if config_flag.is_empty() {
        // Default: cli.yml
        return Ok(paths::config_file());
    }

    let config_flag = if config_flag.starts_with("~/") {
        let home = dirs::home_dir().unwrap_or_default();
        home.join(&config_flag[2..])
    } else {
        PathBuf::from(config_flag)
    };

    // Absolute path - check extension detection
    if config_flag.is_absolute() {
        return Ok(resolve_yaml_extension(&config_flag));
    }

    // Relative path - resolve to configdir
    let path = paths::config_dir().join(&config_flag);

    Ok(resolve_yaml_extension(&path))
}

// resolve_yaml_extension auto-detects .yml or .yaml extension
// If no extension provided, checks for existing .yml then .yaml
// Defaults to .yml if neither exists (for new configs)
fn resolve_yaml_extension(path: &Path) -> PathBuf {
    let ext = path.extension().and_then(|e| e.to_str()).unwrap_or("");

    // Already has yaml extension - use as-is
    if ext == "yml" || ext == "yaml" {
        return path.to_path_buf();
    }

    // No extension - auto-detect
    if ext.is_empty() {
        // Check .yml first
        let yml = path.with_extension("yml");
        if yml.exists() {
            return yml;
        }
        // Check .yaml second
        let yaml = path.with_extension("yaml");
        if yaml.exists() {
            return yaml;
        }
        // Default to .yml for new configs
        return path.with_extension("yml");
    }

    // Non-yaml extension - use as-is (user knows what they're doing)
    path.to_path_buf()
}
```

**Example usage:**
```bash
# Use different configs for different environments
# Uses ~/.config/.../dev.yml
{project_name}-cli --config dev list
# Uses ~/.config/.../staging.yml
{project_name}-cli --config staging list
# Uses absolute path
{project_name}-cli --config ~/work/prod.yml list

# Config profiles allow different servers/tokens without flags
# dev.yml:   server: https://dev.example.com, token: dev-token
# staging.yml: server: https://staging.example.com, token: staging-token
```

### cli.yml Configuration

**EVERYTHING must be configurable via cli.yml. Sane defaults match server where applicable.**

```yaml
# ~/.config/{project_org}/{internal_name}/cli.yml
# client configuration - ALL options with defaults

# Server connection
server:
  # Server URL (empty = use {official_site} or prompt)
  primary: ""
  # API version prefix (default: v1, must match server)
  api_version: v1
  # Request timeout (match server default)
  timeout: 30s
  # Retry attempts on failure
  retry: 3
  # Delay between retries
  retry_delay: 1s

# Authentication
auth:
  # API token (see PART 11)
  token: ""
  # Read token from file instead
  token_file: ""

# Output preferences
output:
  # Default: table, json, yaml, plain, csv
  format: table
  # auto, yes, no (match terminal detection)
  color: auto
  # auto, always, never (use less/more for long output)
  pager: auto
  # Suppress non-essential output
  quiet: false
  # Extra output (same as --verbose)
  verbose: false

# TUI preferences (if TUI supported)
tui:
  # Allow TUI mode (false = CLI-only)
  enabled: true
  # dark, light, system (match server theme options)
  theme: dark
  # Enable mouse support
  mouse: true
  # Use unicode characters (false = ASCII only)
  unicode: true

# Logging
logging:
  # debug, info, warn, error (match server default)
  level: info
  # Log file path (empty = {log_dir}/cli.log)
  file: ""
  # Max log file size (match server default)
  max_size: 10MB
  # Max log files to keep (match server default)
  max_files: 5

# Cache
cache:
  # Enable response caching
  enabled: true
  # Cache TTL (5 minutes)
  ttl: 5m
  # Max cache size
  max_size: 100MB

# Debug
# Enable debug mode (same as --debug)
debug: false

# Project-specific defaults (flag defaults)
defaults:
  # These become defaults for project-specific flags
  # Example for pastebin:
  # public: unlisted               # --public default
  # expire: 24h                    # --expire default
  # syntax: auto                   # --syntax default
```

**Config precedence (highest to lowest):**

| Priority | Source | Example |
|----------|--------|---------|
| 1 | CLI flag | `--format json` |
| 2 | Environment variable | `{PROJECT_NAME}_FORMAT=json` |
| 3 | Config file | `output.format: json` |
| 4 | Compiled default | `table` |

**Environment variable mapping:**
```bash
# Pattern: {PROJECT_NAME}_{SECTION}_{KEY} or {PROJECT_NAME}_{KEY}
{PROJECT_NAME}_SERVER_PRIMARY="https://example.com"
{PROJECT_NAME}_SERVER_TIMEOUT=60
{PROJECT_NAME}_TOKEN="tok_abc123..."
{PROJECT_NAME}_OUTPUT_FORMAT="json"
{PROJECT_NAME}_DEBUG=true
```

### Server Address Resolution

**Priority order (highest to lowest):**

1. `--server` flag (if provided)
2. `server.primary` in `cli.yml` (if set)
3. `{official_site}` compiled default (if project has official site)
4. Error with setup instructions

**Behavior:**

| Scenario | Result |
|----------|--------|
| `--server` flag used | Use that server, **save to `server.primary` in cli.yml** |
| `server.primary` in cli.yml | Use configured primary server |
| Project has `{official_site}` | Use official site as default |
| No server available | Error with setup instructions |

**Config save rule: Validate everything, only save valid, never clear valid.**

```rust
// save_if_empty saves value to config only if current is empty and new is valid
fn save_if_empty(
    current: &str,
    new_value: &str,
    validate: impl Fn(&str) -> bool,
) -> anyhow::Result<String> {
    if new_value.is_empty() {
        return Ok(current.to_string());
    }
    if !validate(new_value) {
        return Err(anyhow::anyhow!("invalid value: {}", new_value));
    }
    if current.is_empty() {
        // Save to config
        return Ok(new_value.to_string());
    }
    // Use for session, don't save (current preserved)
    Ok(new_value.to_string())
}
```

| Principle | Rule |
|-----------|------|
| **Validate everything** | Always validate before use or save |
| **Only save valid** | Never save invalid values to config |
| **Never clear valid** | Don't overwrite existing valid config |

**Error when no server configured (projects without official site):**

```bash
$ {project_name}-cli list
Error: no server configured

To configure a server, run:
  {project_name}-cli --server https://your-server.example.com list

This will save the server address for future commands.
Or edit ~/.config/{project_org}/{internal_name}/cli.yml directly.
```

**Projects with official site show default in help:**

```bash
$ {project_name}-cli --help
...
Flags:
      --server string    Server address (default: https://{project_name}.example.com)
...
```

**Official site (`{official_site}`) is taken from `site.txt` when that file exists. Otherwise fall back to `IDEA.md ## Project variables` (`official_site`) and other compatibility sources.**

**What official site affects:**
- README.md: Default production site URL in examples
- CLI/Agent: Default `--server` URL (so users don't need to specify)

**What official site does NOT affect:**
- Docker labels (use `{project_org}`, `{project_name}`, `{fqdn}`)
- Documentation structure or content
- Build artifacts or binary metadata
- Any runtime behavior (just a compiled default)

Sources for official site (check in order):
1. `site.txt`: single line URL `https://...`
2. `IDEA.md ## Project variables`: `official_site: https://...`
3. README.md: `Official site is: https://...` or `Site: https://...` (compatibility fallback only)
4. AI asks user (if not found in canonical project files)
5. If user selects "none", project has no default server

**Conflict rule:** if both `site.txt` and `IDEA.md` exist and disagree, `site.txt` wins. Update `IDEA.md` to match or remove the stale `official_site` entry.

**AI should ask if not defined:**
```
Official site not found in site.txt, IDEA.md, or README.md.
What is the official site for this project?

1) https://projectname.example.com
2) https://api.example.com/projectname
3) None (users must configure with --server)
4) Other (specify URL)

Enter choice [1-4]:
```

## Standard Flags

### Universal Flags (ALL Binaries)

| Flag | Short | Description |
|------|-------|-------------|
| `--help` | `-h` | Show help |
| `--version` | `-v` | Show version |
| `--color {auto\|yes\|no}` | — | Color output (default: auto, respects `NO_COLOR`) |
| `--lang CODE` | — | Language for output (default: auto-detect from `LANG` env) |

**Only `-h` and `-v` have short forms. Everything else is long-form only.**

- **No escalation** — help at every level (main, subcommand, nested) must never call `sudo`, require root/admin, or check privilege state; exit immediately with the help text.

### Boolean/Truthy-Falsey Handling (CLI & Agent)

**CLI and Agent binaries use the SAME truthy/falsey parsing as the server.**

See PART 5: Boolean Handling for the complete implementation.

| Truthy | Falsey |
|--------|--------|
| `true`, `yes`, `on`, `1`, `enable`, `enabled` | `false`, `no`, `off`, `0`, `disable`, `disabled`, `none` |

**Usage in flags:**
```bash
# Boolean flag (true)
{project_name}-cli --public
# Explicit truthy
{project_name}-cli --public=yes
# Explicit falsey
{project_name}-cli --public=no
# Falsey = no expiration
{project_name}-cli --expire=0
# Falsey = no expiration
{project_name}-cli --expire=disabled
```

**Config file (cli.yml):**
```yaml
server:
  # Truthy
  verify_ssl: yes
  # Falsey
  auto_update: false
  # Truthy
  notifications: enabled
```

**ALL boolean inputs MUST use `config::parse_bool()` or `config::is_truthy()` - NEVER Rust's built-in bool parsing.**

### Common Optional Flags (if needed)

| Flag | When to Include |
|------|-----------------|
| `--server` | Multi-server support (skip if single official site) |
| `--token` | Authenticated operations |
| `--config` | Multiple config profiles |
| `--output` | Multiple output formats (json/table/plain) |
| `--debug` | Debug mode |

### Flag Argument Syntax (ALL Binaries)

**All binaries accept BOTH `=` and space for flag values:**
```
--{flag}={value}    # Equals syntax
--{flag} {value}    # Space syntax
```

**Examples:**
```bash
--public=yes    OR  --public yes
--expire=24h    OR  --expire 24h
--limit=10      OR  --limit 10
--output=json   OR  --output json
--color=yes  OR  --color yes
```

### Project-Specific Flags (by service type)

**Search/Query CLI (minimal flags):**
```bash
# Args ARE the search - no flags needed for basic use
# Search
{project_name}-cli rust tutorials
# With limit
{project_name}-cli --limit 10 rust
# JSON output
{project_name}-cli --output json rust
```

**Pastebin/Content CLI:**
```bash
# Smart detection handles input, flags for metadata
# File (detected), uses defaults
{project_name}-cli notes.txt
# Public paste
{project_name}-cli notes.txt --public yes
# Private (requires auth)
{project_name}-cli notes.txt --public no
# Unlisted (default)
{project_name}-cli notes.txt --public unlisted
# Expiration
{project_name}-cli notes.txt --expire 24h
# Syntax highlight
{project_name}-cli notes.txt --syntax python
# Author name
{project_name}-cli notes.txt --author "John"
```

**API/Data CLI:**
```bash
# Resource-specific flags
# Get by ID
{project_name}-cli get abc123
# Pagination
{project_name}-cli list --limit 20 --offset 0
# Dangerous ops need confirm
{project_name}-cli delete abc123 --force
```

### Flag Defaults from Config

**Every flag MUST have a corresponding config setting to change its default.**

```yaml
# cli.yml - defaults for flags
defaults:
  # --lang default (auto = detect from env, or "en", "es", etc.)
  lang: auto
  # --public default (yes, no, unlisted)
  public: unlisted
  # --expire default
  expire: 24h
  # --syntax default
  syntax: auto
  # --output default (json, table, plain)
  output: table
  # --limit default
  limit: 20
```

**Precedence (highest to lowest):**
1. Command-line flag (`--public yes`)
2. Environment variable (`{PROJECT_NAME}_PUBLIC=yes`)
3. Config file (`defaults.public: yes`)
4. Hardcoded default

### Flag Guidelines

| Rule | Description |
|------|-------------|
| **Minimize flags** | Smart detection > explicit flags |
| **Config defaults** | Every flag has config equivalent |
| **No redundant flags** | If detectable, don't require flag |
| **Value flags** | `--public unlisted` not `--unlisted` |
| **Confirmation** | Destructive ops: `--force` or interactive confirm |

### Shell Completions (Built-in, NON-NEGOTIABLE)

**ALL binaries (server, client) MUST support shell completions. Built into binary - no separate files.**

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `--shell completions [SHELL]` | Print completion script to stdout |
| `--shell init [SHELL]` | Alias: prints eval-wrapped completions command |

**`[SHELL]` is OPTIONAL - both commands auto-detect from `$SHELL` env var if omitted.**

**`--shell init` is just a convenience wrapper:**
```bash
# These are equivalent:
eval "$({project_name} --shell init)"
# if $SHELL=/bin/bash
eval "$({project_name} --shell init bash)"

# init outputs the eval command, completions outputs the script:
# → source <({project_name} --shell completions bash)
{project_name} --shell init
# → (actual completion script)
{project_name} --shell completions
```

**Supported shells:**

| Shell | Completion Support | Init Support |
|-------|-------------------|--------------|
| `bash` | Full | `source <(...)` |
| `zsh` | Full | `source <(...)` |
| `fish` | Full | `... \| source` |
| `sh` | Basic (POSIX) | `eval $(...)` |
| `dash` | Basic (POSIX) | `eval $(...)` |
| `ksh` | Basic | `eval $(...)` |
| `powershell` | Full | `Invoke-Expression` |
| `pwsh` | Full (PowerShell Core) | `Invoke-Expression` |

**Behavior:**
- `--shell completions` prints to stdout - user redirects with `> {file}`
- `--shell init` prints eval-ready command for shell rc file
- Auto-detect extracts shell name from `$SHELL` path (e.g., `/bin/bash` → `bash`)

**Usage examples:**
```bash
# Explicit shell specification
{project_name} --shell completions bash > ~/.local/share/bash-completion/completions/{project_name}
{project_name}-cli --shell completions zsh > ~/.zsh/completions/_{project_name}-cli
{project_name} --shell completions powershell > ~/Documents/PowerShell/completions/{project_name}.ps1

# Auto-detect shell (omit SHELL argument)
{project_name} --shell completions > ~/completions/{project_name}
# auto-detect, print init
{project_name}-cli --shell init
# auto-detect in eval
eval "$({project_name} --shell init)"

# Specific shell init
eval "$({project_name}-cli --shell init bash)"
{project_name} --shell init fish | source
```

**Add to shell rc file:**
```bash
# ~/.bashrc, ~/.zshrc, ~/.config/fish/config.fish, etc.
# server (auto-detect)
eval "$({project_name} --shell init)"
# client (auto-detect)
eval "$({project_name}-cli --shell init)"
```

**Why built-in (not separate files):**

| Advantage | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| **Always current** | Completions match binary version exactly |
| **Rename-friendly** | Works even if user renames binary |
| **No sync issues** | Can't have outdated completion files |
| **Self-contained** | No extra files to distribute/install |

**Implementation:**
```rust
fn handle_shell_command(args: &[String]) {
    if args.is_empty() {
        eprintln!("Usage: --shell [completions|init] [SHELL]");
        std::process::exit(1);
    }

    let cmd = &args[0];
    let shell = if args.len() > 1 {
        args[1].clone()
    } else {
        // auto-detect from $SHELL
        detect_shell()
    };

    let binary_name = std::env::args().next()
        .and_then(|p| std::path::Path::new(&p).file_name().map(|n| n.to_string_lossy().into_owned()))
        .unwrap_or_default();

    match cmd.as_str() {
        "completions" => print_completions(&shell, &binary_name),
        "init" => print_init(&shell, &binary_name),
        _ => {}
    }
}

fn detect_shell() -> String {
    let shell_path = std::env::var("SHELL").unwrap_or_default();
    if shell_path.is_empty() {
        // default fallback
        return "bash".to_string();
    }
    std::path::Path::new(&shell_path)
        .file_name()
        .map(|n| n.to_string_lossy().into_owned())
        .unwrap_or_else(|| "bash".to_string())
}

fn print_completions(shell: &str, binary_name: &str) {
    match shell {
        "bash" => print!("{}", generate_bash_completions(binary_name)),
        "zsh" => print!("{}", generate_zsh_completions(binary_name)),
        "fish" => print!("{}", generate_fish_completions(binary_name)),
        "sh" | "dash" | "ksh" => print!("{}", generate_posix_completions(binary_name)),
        "powershell" | "pwsh" => print!("{}", generate_powershell_completions(binary_name)),
        _ => {
            eprintln!("{}", i18n::tf(lang, "cli.error_unsupported_shell", shell));
            std::process::exit(1);
        }
    }
}

fn print_init(shell: &str, binary_name: &str) {
    match shell {
        "bash" => println!("source <({} --shell completions bash)", binary_name),
        "zsh" => println!("source <({} --shell completions zsh)", binary_name),
        "fish" => println!("{} --shell completions fish | source", binary_name),
        "sh" | "dash" | "ksh" => println!("eval \"$({} --shell completions {})\"", binary_name, shell),
        "powershell" | "pwsh" => println!("Invoke-Expression (& {} --shell completions powershell)", binary_name),
        _ => {
            eprintln!("{}", i18n::tf(lang, "cli.error_unsupported_shell", shell));
            std::process::exit(1);
        }
    }
}
```

### --help Output

```bash
$ {project_name}-cli --help
{project_name}-cli {project_version} - CLI for {project_name}

Usage:
  {project_name}-cli [args] [flags]
  # TUI mode (no args)
  {project_name}-cli

Flags:
-h, --help                             - Show help
-v, --version                          - Show version
--shell completions [SHELL]            - Print shell completions (auto-detect if SHELL omitted)
--shell init [SHELL]                   - Print shell init command (auto-detect if SHELL omitted)
--shell help                           - Show shell integration help

--server URL                           - Server URL (default: from config)
--token TOKEN                          - API token for authentication
--token-file FILE                      - Read token from file
--config NAME                          - Config profile name (default: cli.yml)
--debug                                - Debug output
--color {auto|yes|no}                  - Color output (default: auto)
--lang CODE                            - Language for output (default: auto)

  {project-specific flags listed here}

Shells: bash, zsh, fish, sh, dash, ksh, powershell, pwsh

Run without arguments for interactive TUI mode.
Run '{project_name}-cli <command> help' for detailed help on any command.
```

**If user renames binary:**
```bash
$ mypaste --help
# Shows actual binary name
mypaste {project_version} - client for {project_name} API

Usage:
  # Shows actual binary name
  mypaste [command] [flags]
...
```

### --version Output

**MUST match server `--version` format. Shows ACTUAL binary name:**

```bash
$ {project_name}-cli --version
{project_name}-cli {project_version} ({commit_sha}) built {build_date}

# If renamed:
$ mypaste --version
# Shows actual name
mypaste {project_version} ({commit_sha}) built {build_date}
```

Same format as server:
```bash
$ pastebin --version
pastebin {project_version} ({commit_sha}) built {build_date}
```

## Commands

**clients have project-specific commands only.** No standard commands required.

Example commands (project-dependent):
- `create`, `get`, `list`, `delete` (pastebin)
- `lookup`, `search`, `nearby` (airports)
- `random`, `search` (quotes)

**Notes:**
- Version and help are FLAGS (`--version`/`-v`, `--help`/`-h`), NOT commands
- No `config` command - edit `cli.yml` directly or use `--server`/`--token` flags
- No `tui` command - TUI launches automatically when no arguments provided
- Config file (`cli.yml`) is auto-created on first run with sane defaults

## Authentication

**Authentication requirements are PROJECT-DEPENDENT and ROUTE-DEPENDENT.**

| Auth Type | When Used |
|-----------|-----------|
| None | Public endpoints (e.g., `GET /api/{api_version}/pastes/{id}`) |
| Resource Owner Token | Protected endpoints scoped to a single resource |
| Operator Token (`server.token`) | Server-wide operator actions (CLI only) |

**Token Storage:**
- Stored in `cli.yml` under `server.token`
- `--token` flag saves to cli.yml only if not already set (same as `--server`)
- Environment variable: `{PROJECT_NAME}_CLI_TOKEN` (does NOT save to config)

**Priority (highest to lowest):**
1. `--token` flag (saves only if config empty/invalid)
2. `{PROJECT_NAME}_CLI_TOKEN` environment variable
3. `server.token` in cli.yml

## HTTP Client Identity

### User-Agent Rule

**The CLI binary can be renamed by users, but the User-Agent MUST always use the original project name.**

| Aspect | Uses Filename | Uses Original Name |
|--------|---------------|-------------------|
| Display in `--help` | ✓ | |
| Display in `--version` | ✓ | |
| Error messages | ✓ | |
| **Config file path** | | ✓ |
| **User-Agent header** | | ✓ |

**Why?** The server uses User-Agent to identify client types for:
- Client-specific rate limits
- Client-specific settings/features
- Analytics and usage tracking
- Version compatibility checks

### User-Agent Format

```
{project_name}-cli/{version}
```

**Examples (User-Agent uses compiled project name, not binary name):**

| Binary Name | User-Agent Header |
|-------------|-------------------|
| `{project_name}-cli` | `{project_name}-cli/1.2.3` |
| `mypaste` (renamed by user) | `{project_name}-cli/1.2.3` |
| `pb` (renamed by user) | `{project_name}-cli/1.2.3` |

### Implementation

The project name is compiled into the binary at build time:

```rust
// Set at build time via env! macro or build.rs
// set via build.rs or --cfg
const PROJECT_NAME: &str = env!("PROJECT_NAME");
const VERSION: &str = env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION");

// get_user_agent returns the fixed User-Agent regardless of binary name
pub fn get_user_agent() -> String {
    format!("{}-cli/{}", PROJECT_NAME, VERSION)
}

// get_binary_name returns the actual executable name (for display)
pub fn get_binary_name() -> String {
    std::env::args().next()
        .and_then(|p| std::path::Path::new(&p).file_name().map(|n| n.to_string_lossy().into_owned()))
        .unwrap_or_default()
}
```

**Build command (CI/CD injects version from git tag):**
```bash
# VERSION comes from git tag (see PART 25/28 for version handling)
cargo build --release --target x86_64-unknown-linux-musl
```

### Server-Side Client Detection

The server can use the User-Agent to apply client-specific behavior:

```rust
fn get_client_type(headers: &HeaderMap) -> &'static str {
    let ua = headers.get("User-Agent")
        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())
        .unwrap_or("");
    if ua.starts_with(&format!("{}-cli/", PROJECT_NAME)) {
        return "cli";
    }
    if ua.contains("Mozilla") || ua.contains("Chrome") {
        return "browser";
    }
    "api"
}
```

## URL Encoding

**ALL user input in URLs MUST be encoded. NEVER construct URLs with raw user input.**

### URL Encoding Functions (Reusable)

```rust
// src/common/urlutil/encode.rs

use url::Url;
use std::collections::HashMap;

// build_api_url constructs a properly encoded API URL
// ALWAYS use this function - NEVER format! with user input
pub fn build_api_url(
    base_url: &str,
    path: &str,
    path_params: &HashMap<&str, &str>,
    query_params: &HashMap<&str, &str>,
) -> String {
```
